question_text: Let $\{ X _ { n } \} _ { n \ge 1 }$ be a sequence of independent and identically distributed random variables each having probability density function $ f ( x ) = { \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { e ^ { - x } , } & { x > 0 , } \\ { 0 , } & { { \mathrm { o t h e r w i s e } } . } \end{array} \right. } $ Let $X _ { ( n ) } = \operatorname* { m a x } \{ X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , \ldots , X _ { n } \}$ for $n \geq 1$ . If Z is the random variable to which $\{ X _ { ( n ) } - \log n \} _ { n \geq 1 }$ converges in distribution, as $n \to \infty$ , then the median of Z equals (round off to
solution:
To find the median of $Z ,$ we examine the behavior of $X _ { ( n ) }$ and the limiting distribution of Z as $n \to \infty$ . Using standard results for the distribution of the maximum of independent identically distributed random variables, we find that the median of $Z$ is approximately:

 $ \mathbf { M e d i a n } ( Z ) \approx 0 . 3 2 . $ 

Thus, the value is 0.32.
Quick Tip

To solve problems involving the maximum of independent random variables, use the properties of the distribution and the limiting behavior of the sequence.
